skill.name: Azure Monitor Query SDK for Python
skill.description: Query Azure Monitor logs and metrics using the official Azure Monitor Query SDK for Python with Log Analytics and Metrics Query clients
skill.summary: Query Log Analytics workspaces and Azure Monitor metrics with the official Python SDK
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation and code examples for the Azure Monitor Query SDK for Python. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code, resulting in a risk score of 0/100. The skill is prompt-only with no executable code, containing reference documentation for querying Log Analytics workspaces and Azure Monitor metrics. Environment variable access is documented for Azure authentication but poses no security risk as these are standard Azure SDK configuration patterns.

content.user_title: Query Azure Monitor logs and metrics with Python SDK
content.value_statement: Developers struggle to query Azure Monitor data efficiently without proper SDK guidance. This skill provides ready-to-use code examples for Log Analytics and Metrics Query clients with authentication patterns.
actual_capabilities: Generate Kusto queries for Log Analytics workspaces
Query Azure Monitor metrics with aggregation options
Handle batch queries for multiple workspaces
Convert query results to pandas DataFrames
Use async clients for non-blocking operations
Filter metrics by dimensions and time ranges
limitations: Requires valid Azure credentials and workspace configuration
Does not execute queries directly - provides code examples only
Limited to Azure Monitor data sources, not other Azure services
Requires the azure-monitor-query Python package to be installed

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Monitoring Application Health
use_cases.description: Query application request rates, error counts, and response times from Log Analytics to create dashboards and alerts for production monitoring

use_cases.title: Data Analyst Investigating Performance Issues
use_cases.description: Run Kusto queries to identify slow requests, exceptions, and bottlenecks in application performance data stored in Azure Monitor

use_cases.title: Cloud Architect Analyzing Resource Utilization
use_cases.description: Query Azure Monitor metrics to analyze CPU usage, network traffic, and resource consumption patterns across Azure infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Basic Log Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Kusto query to count AppRequests grouped by ResultCode for the last hour using LogsQueryClient in Python
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Simple query to get started with Log Analytics

prompt_templates.title: Time Range Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Query Log Analytics for AppExceptions over a specific date range from January 1-2, 2024 using Python with proper timezone handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Working with specific time windows

prompt_templates.title: Metrics with Aggregations
prompt_templates.prompt: Query Azure Monitor metrics for CPU percentage with Average, Maximum, and Minimum aggregations using MetricsQueryClient with 5-minute granularity
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Combining metrics with multiple aggregation types

prompt_templates.title: Batch Query Multiple Workspaces
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute batch queries across multiple Log Analytics workspaces to retrieve AppRequests and AppExceptions simultaneously, handling partial results and errors gracefully
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Production-ready batch operations with error handling

output_examples.input: Query workspace for top 10 requests by duration
output: Code generated using LogsQueryClient with query: AppRequests | top 10 by DurationMs
Response iterates through tables and rows to print request data
Includes timedelta for time range configuration

output_examples.input: Get CPU metrics for a VM with 5-minute intervals
output: Code using MetricsQueryClient with resource URI from environment
Queries Percentage CPU metric with Average aggregation
Loops through timeseries data to print timestamp and values

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for secure authentication that supports multiple credential types
Set appropriate time ranges and granularity to limit data volume and improve query performance
Handle partial results and failures gracefully using LogsQueryStatus checks
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode workspace IDs or resource URIs - always use environment variables
Avoid querying without time bounds as this can return excessive data and incur high costs
Do not ignore partial results - always check LogsQueryStatus before processing response

faq.question: What Azure permissions do I need to query Log Analytics?
faq.answer: You need Log Analytics Reader role on the workspace or higher permissions. For metrics, you need Reader access to the resource.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure Monitor Query SDK?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically tries multiple authentication methods including managed identity, Azure CLI, and environment credentials.

faq.question: What is the cost of querying Log Analytics?
faq.answer: Log Analytics charges per GB of data scanned. Optimize queries with time ranges, filters, and projection to reduce costs.

faq.question: Can I query multiple workspaces in a single request?
faq.answer: Yes, use the batch query feature with LogsBatchQuery objects to run multiple queries efficiently in one API call.

faq.question: What time formats does the SDK accept?
faq.answer: Use Python timedelta for relative times (eg, last hour) or datetime objects with timezone info for absolute time ranges.

faq.question: How do I handle async operations with the SDK?
faq.answer: Use the aio module clients (LogsQueryClient, MetricsQueryClient) with async/await syntax and remember to close clients and credentials.
